mitigations.mitigation_name: Insert a Snort inline IPS on the LAN side just after the FortiGate firewall (between the firewall and internal hosts) so it can automatically detect and block ICMP flooding patterns and high-rate ICMP flows in real time.

Segment the compromised UbuntuComputer into a restricted VLAN/zone (micro-segmentation) and enforce firewall policies that only permit minimal required egress (management/monitoring) while denying bulk/unsanctioned protocols; keep the host on the network but limit its ability to send high-volume ICMP externally.

args.command:
config firewall address
edit "UbuntuComputer"
 set subnet 192.168.1.3 255.255.255.255
next
edit "Caldera_Server"
 set subnet 203.0.113.1 255.255.255.255
next
end

config firewall service custom
edit "CALDERA_8443"
 set protocol TCP
 set tcp-portrange 8443
next
edit "DNS_udp"
 set protocol UDP
 set udp-portrange 53
next
end

config firewall policy
edit 1
 set name "Deny_Ubuntu_ICMP"
 set srcintf "port2"
 set dstintf "port1"
 set srcaddr "UbuntuComputer"
 set dstaddr "all"
 set action deny
 set service "PING"
 set schedule "always"
 set logtraffic all
next
edit 2
 set name "Allow_Ubuntu_Caldera_8443"
 set srcintf "port2"
 set dstintf "port1"
 set srcaddr "UbuntuComputer"
 set dstaddr "Caldera_Server"
 set action accept
 set service "CALDERA_8443"
 set nat enable
 set schedule "always"
 set logtraffic all
next
edit 3
 set name "Allow_Ubuntu_DNS"
 set srcintf "port2"
 set dstintf "port1"
 set srcaddr "UbuntuComputer"
 set dstaddr "all"
 set action accept
 set service "DNS_udp"
 set schedule "always"
 set logtraffic all
next
end

config firewall policy
show
end

config firewall address
show
end
